certifications.name: Microsoft Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900)
certifications.description: Covers foundational knowledge of Azure cloud services, security, privacy, compliance, trust, and pricing models.

cheatsheet.summary: Foundational Azure credentials validating core geography, compute structures, Blob storage, Microsoft Entra ID authentication, subscription controls, and resource locks.

coreConcepts.name: Azure Core Geographies
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Regions (geographical areas), Availability Zones (isolated datacenters within a region), Region Pairs (cross-region replication pairs for DR).

coreConcepts.name: Compute & Web Resources
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Virtual Machines (VMs), Azure App Services (PaaS web apps), Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Azure Virtual Desktop.

coreConcepts.name: Blob & Storage Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Blob Storage (unstructured object storage), Azure Files (managed network file shares), Azure Disk Storage (block storage for VMs).

coreConcepts.name: Microsoft Entra ID Security
coreConcepts.desc: Provides identity access management (formerly Azure Active Directory), Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), MFA, and Conditional Access policies.

coreConcepts.name: Azure Governance & Resource Locks
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Policies enforce configuration standards. Resource Locks (CannotDelete, ReadOnly) prevent accidental deletions of critical database resource groups.

coreConcepts.name: Management & Architecture Tools
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Portal, Azure CLI, Azure Cloud Shell, Azure Resource Manager (ARM templates define declarative infrastructure deployments).

commands.desc: Launches a browser session to authenticate and log in the active Azure CLI session.
Initializes a logical resource group container in the specified location.
Provisions a virtual machine inside the resource group.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Declare Virtual Networks, Subnets, and SQL Databases inside an ARM or Bicep template to guarantee consistent deployments across Dev/Prod environments.
Register a PaaS App Service in Microsoft Entra ID. Assign an RBAC Reader role to the service account, and enforce Conditional Access MFA rules.

flashcards.back:
- Azure Regions: geographical areas containing datacenters
- Availability Zones: physically separate datacenters inside a region
- Azure Region Pairs: direct cross-region DR failover mappings
- Azure Subscriptions: logical billing boundary for cloud resources

- Compute: Azure VMs (IaaS), App Service (PaaS), AKS (containers)
- Storage: Blob storage (objects), Azure Files (network), Disk storage
- Network: Virtual Networks (VNets), ExpressRoute (private Interconnect)

- Microsoft Entra ID: central identity and directory service
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): assign resource permissions
- Conditional Access: MFA policies based on user login location
- Microsoft Defender for Cloud: unified security management

- Azure Policies: enforce configuration compliance standards
- Azure Resource Manager (ARM): deploy Bicep/ARM templates
- Resource Locks: prevent critical resource deletions (CannotDelete)
- Azure Service Health: dashboard for system outage alerts

study_flashcards.back:
- Region: Geographic area with one or more data centers
- Availability Zone: Physically separate data center within a region
- 60+ regions worldwide, most with 3 AZs
- Region Pairs: Two regions paired for disaster recovery
- Sovereign regions: US Gov, China (21Vianet)
- Geography: Contains 2+ regions for data residency compliance

study_flashcards.front: IaaS vs PaaS vs SaaS on Azure?
study_flashcards.back:
- IaaS: Virtual Machines, Virtual Networks, Storage Accounts
- PaaS: App Service, Azure SQL, Azure Functions
- SaaS: Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, Power Platform
- IaaS: You manage OS, runtime, apps
- PaaS: You manage apps and data only
- SaaS: You manage data and access configuration only

- Virtual Machines: Full IaaS, most control
- App Service: PaaS web hosting, auto-scaling
- Azure Functions: Serverless, event-driven, pay per execution
- Container Instances (ACI): Serverless containers
- Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS): Managed Kubernetes
- Virtual Desktop: Cloud-hosted Windows desktops

- Blob Storage: Unstructured data (hot, cool, cold, archive tiers)
- File Storage: Managed file shares (SMB/NFS protocol)
- Queue Storage: Message queuing between components
- Table Storage: NoSQL key-value store
- Disk Storage: Managed disks for VMs (Standard HDD/SSD, Premium SSD, Ultra)
- Data Lake Storage Gen2: Hierarchical namespace for analytics

- Hot: Frequently accessed, highest storage cost, lowest access cost
- Cool: Infrequently accessed, 30-day minimum, lower storage cost
- Cold: Rarely accessed, 90-day minimum
- Archive: Offline, 180-day minimum, hours to rehydrate
- Lifecycle management policies auto-transition between tiers
- Set at account level (default) or blob level

- Virtual Network (VNet): Isolated network in Azure (regional)
- Subnets: Segments within a VNet
- Network Security Groups (NSGs): Filter traffic by rules
- Azure Firewall: Managed network security service
- VPN Gateway: Encrypted connection to on-premises
- ExpressRoute: Private, dedicated connection to Azure

- Cloud-based identity and access management service
- SSO to thousands of SaaS applications
- Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
- Conditional Access: Policy-based access control
- B2B: External user collaboration
- B2C: Customer identity management
- Replaces Azure Active Directory branding

- Microsoft Defender for Cloud: Security posture management
- Azure Sentinel: Cloud-native SIEM and SOAR
- Key Vault: Manage secrets, keys, and certificates
- DDoS Protection: Standard tier for enhanced protection
- Azure Information Protection: Classify and protect data
- Microsoft Secure Score: Security improvement recommendations

- Management Groups: Organize subscriptions
- Subscriptions: Billing and access boundary
- Resource Groups: Logical container for resources
- Resources: Individual services (VMs, DBs, etc.)
- RBAC: Role-based access control at any level
- Azure Policy: Enforce organizational standards
- Blueprints: Repeatable environment templates

- Pricing Calculator: Estimate costs before deployment
- TCO Calculator: Compare on-prem vs Azure costs
- Cost Management: Monitor and analyze spending
- Advisor: Cost optimization recommendations
- Reserved Instances: 1-3 year commit for savings
- Spot VMs: Unused capacity at deep discounts
- Azure Hybrid Benefit: Use existing Windows/SQL licenses

- Trust Center: Compliance documentation hub
- Service Trust Portal: Audit reports and certifications
- 100+ compliance offerings (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC)
- Azure compliance documentation: Region-specific requirements
- Compliance Manager: Track compliance progress
- Data residency: Control where data is stored

- Azure Portal: Web-based GUI
- Azure CLI: Cross-platform command line (bash-style)
- Azure PowerShell: Windows PowerShell cmdlets
- Azure Cloud Shell: Browser-based CLI (Bash or PowerShell)
- Azure Mobile App: Monitor and manage on-the-go
- ARM Templates / Bicep: Infrastructure as Code
- Azure Arc: Manage multi-cloud and on-prem resources

- Azure SQL Database: Managed SQL Server (PaaS)
- Azure SQL Managed Instance: Near 100% SQL Server compatibility
- Cosmos DB: Globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL
- Azure Database for MySQL/PostgreSQL: Managed open-source DBs
- Azure Cache for Redis: In-memory caching
- Cosmos DB: Single-digit ms latency, 99.999% availability

- Azure AI Services: Pre-built APIs (vision, speech, language)
- Azure OpenAI Service: GPT-4, DALL-E, Whisper access
- Azure Machine Learning: End-to-end ML platform
- Azure Bot Service: Build conversational AI bots
- Cognitive Search: AI-powered search with indexing
- Form Recognizer: Extract data from documents

- Azure Monitor: Metrics, logs, alerts, diagnostics
- Application Insights: App performance monitoring (APM)
- Log Analytics: Query and analyze log data (KQL)
- Service Health: Track Azure service incidents
- Azure Advisor: Best practice recommendations
- Support plans: Basic (free), Developer, Standard, Professional Direct

questions.question: [Azure Cloud Portal Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the name of Microsoft Azure's administrative portal?
questions.explanation: The Azure Portal is a web-based, unified console that provides an alternative to command-line tools. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q1)

questions.question: [Azure Resource Groups Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which Azure resource container group holds related resources that share the same lifecycle, permissions, and policies?
questions.explanation: A Resource Group is a logical container for resources deployed on Azure. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q2)

questions.question: [Azure Connectivity Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: An organization wants to configure a private connection between its physical office network and its Azure Virtual Network. The connection must not use the public internet. Which service should they choose?
questions.explanation: Azure ExpressRoute lets you extend your on-premises networks into the Microsoft cloud over a private connection. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q3)

questions.question: [Azure VMs Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which service represents Azure's primary Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) virtual machines?
questions.explanation: Azure Virtual Machines provide on-demand, scalable computing resources in the form of virtualized server hardware. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q4)

questions.question: [Azure Blob Storage Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What Azure storage service is optimized for storing massive amounts of unstructured object data, such as audio, video, or backup logs?

questions.question: [Azure Cloud Portal Scenario 3] In your company's Project C environment: What is the name of Microsoft Azure's administrative portal?
questions.explanation: The Azure Portal is a web-based, unified console that provides an alternative to command-line tools. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q11)

questions.question: [Azure Resource Groups Scenario 3] In your company's Project C environment: Which Azure resource container group holds related resources that share the same lifecycle, permissions, and policies?
questions.explanation: A Resource Group is a logical container for resources deployed on Azure. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q12)

questions.question: [Azure Connectivity Scenario 3] In your company's Project C environment: An organization wants to configure a private connection between its physical office network and its Azure Virtual Network. The connection must not use the public internet. Which service should they choose?
questions.explanation: Azure ExpressRoute lets you extend your on-premises networks into the Microsoft cloud over a private connection. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q13)

questions.question: [Azure VMs Scenario 3] In your company's Project C environment: Which service represents Azure's primary Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) virtual machines?
questions.explanation: Azure Virtual Machines provide on-demand, scalable computing resources in the form of virtualized server hardware. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q14)

questions.question: [Azure Blob Storage Scenario 3] In your company's Project C environment: What Azure storage service is optimized for storing massive amounts of unstructured object data, such as audio, video, or backup logs?
questions.explanation: Azure Blob Storage is Microsoft's object storage solution for the cloud, optimized for storing massive amounts of unstructured data. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: microsoft-azure-fundamentals-q15)
